summary: sensivity ships hidden persistence dropper, obfuscated payload, and game-cheat native module masquerading as a 'mobile control panel'
details: On require/run of the package, launcher.js (declared as `main`) executes top-level side effects with no consent gate: it sets process.title to 'Runtime Broker' to impersonate a legitimate Windows process, writes an HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run entry named 'OneDriveUpdate' via a spawned PowerShell command pointing to wscript.exe running the shipped OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs, and `eval()`s the contents of server.obf.js — a ~110KB heavily obfuscated blob (obfuscator.io, rotated 501-entry string array). The VBS dropper silently spawns `node.exe launcher.js` with a hidden window (Run..., 0, False), achieving boot persistence under a Microsoft-impersonating name. launcher.js also polls every 3 seconds via PowerShell for a chrome.exe window with a title matching 'YouTube' as an environmental activation gate before surfacing any UI, keeping the tool dormant during casual inspection. The shipped sens.node Windows native module (6.8 MB) contains game-memory symbols ('Teleport', 'LeftFoot', 'LeftHand', 'Head') and public/index.html exposes 'Start Cheat', 'Aimbot', 'Triggerbot', and 'Crosshair' controls — the package is a Roblox-style game cheat presented under a misleading 'Mobile Control Panel' description. Combined behaviors (Microsoft-impersonating persistence, hidden-window VBS dropper, eval of opaque obfuscated code at import, anti-analysis activation gate, undisclosed cheat payload) constitute a backdoor on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
